model: DINOv2 (facebook/dinov2-base), frozen, mean-pool over 81 CLS tokens
evaluation: MIDA — re-run LOSO on target dataset with each fold's training set augmented by the full auxiliary pool (the other 3 datasets); linear probe on mean-pooled features (NOT the BiLSTM from dinov2_loso.py — see module docstring for why). n and computation identical to LOSO (Care-PD paper, sec. 4.2 / Appendix D.4).
note: Feature aggregation: mean over (81, 768) → (768,), identical to dinov2_lodo.py / dinov2_cda.py — chosen because MIDA's training pool mixes heterogeneous cohorts, the same scenario that ruled out the BiLSTM for LODO/CDA. Within-dataset LOSO uses the BiLSTM (dinov2_loso.py); that remains the primary single-cohort evaluation.

rationale.mida: See videomae_mida.py for full rationale (verbatim Care-PD MIDA protocol).
rationale.single_stage_fit: Convex linear probes converge to the same solution regardless of init — a joint fit on (target_train ∪ auxiliary) is mathematically equivalent to 'fine-tuning from a LODO checkpoint'.
rationale.no_sample_weighting: Each LOSO fold's target-train split already contains ~(n-1)/n of the cohort.
rationale.comparison_frame: LODO vs. MIDA vs. LOSO — identical to the framing used in the Care-PD paper.
